title: Understand equal groups as multiplication
description: Practice relating equal groups, repeated addition, and multiplication.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the table to make three equivalent expressions.**

Equivalent expressions ||
:-|
$7+7+7+7$ |
[[â˜ƒ numeric-input 3]]  $ {\text{ sevens}}$|
$4\times$ [[â˜ƒ numeric-input 4]] |
hints: $\overset{\greenD1}{\blueD{7}} + \overset{\greenD2}{\blueD{7}} + \overset{\greenD3}{\blueD{7}} + \overset{\greenD4}{\blueD{7}}$ is the same as $\green4\blueD{\text{ sevens}}$.
$\greenD4\blueD{\text{ sevens}}$ is $\greenD4$ groups of $\blueD7$.
We can write $\greenD4$ groups of $\blueD7$ with multiplication as $\greenD4\times\blueD7$.

Here is the completed table: 

Equivalent expressions ||
:-|
$\blueD7+\blueD7+\blueD7+\blueD7$ |
$\greenD4\blueD{\text{ sevens}}$|
$\greenD4\times\blueD7$ |

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the table to make three equivalent expressions.**

Equivalent expressions ||
:-|
$2+2+2+2+2+2$ |
 [[â˜ƒ numeric-input 7]] $ {\text{ twos}}$|
$6\times$ [[â˜ƒ numeric-input 6]] |
hints: $\overset{\greenD1}{\blueD{2}} + \overset{\greenD2}{\blueD{2}} + \overset{\greenD3}{\blueD{2}} + \overset{\greenD4}{\blueD{2}} + \overset{\greenD5}{\blueD{2}} + \overset{\greenD6}{\blueD{2}}$ is the same as $\green6\blueD{\text{ twos}}$.
$\greenD6\blueD{\text{ twos}}$ is $\greenD6$ groups of $\blueD2$.
We can write $\greenD6$ groups of $\blueD2$ with multiplication as $\greenD6\times\blueD2$.

Here is the completed table: 

Equivalent expressions ||
:-|
$\blueD2+\blueD2+\blueD2+\blueD2+\blueD2 +\blueD2 $ |
$\greenD6\blueD{\text{ twos}}$|
$\greenD6\times\blueD2$ |

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions are equivalent to $\blueD{5}\greenD{\text{ threes}}$?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints: $\greenD5\blueD{\text{ threes}} $ is the same as $\overset{\greenD1}{\blueD{3}} + \overset{\greenD2}{\blueD{3}} + \overset{\greenD3}{\blueD{3}} + \overset{\greenD4}{\blueD{3}} + \overset{\greenD5}{\blueD{3}}$.
We can also think of $\greenD5\blueD{\text{  threes}} $ as $\greenD5$ groups of $\blueD3$.
We can write $\greenD5$ groups of $\blueD3$ with multiplication as $\greenD5\times\blueD3$.

The following expressions are equivalent to $\greenD{5}\blueD{\text{ threes}}$:

- $\blueD3 + \blueD3 + \blueD3 + \blueD3 + \blueD3$
- $\greenD5\times\blueD3$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions are equal to $\greenD{3}\blueD{\text{ nines}}$?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints: $\greenD3\blueD{\text{  nines}} $ is the same as $\overset{\greenD1}{\blueD{9}} + \overset{\greenD2}{\blueD{9}} + \overset{\greenD3}{\blueD{9}}$.
We can also think of $\greenD3\blueD{\text{  nines}} $ as $\greenD3$ groups of $\blueD9$.
We can write $\greenD3$ groups of $\blueD9$ with multiplication as $\greenD3\times\blueD9$.

The following expressions are equivalent to $\greenD{3}\blueD{\text{ nines}}$:

- $\blueD9 + \blueD9 + \blueD9$
- $\greenD3\times\blueD9$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions are equal to $\greenD{7}\blueD{\text{ sixes}}$?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints: $\greenD7\blueD{\text{ sixes}} $ is the same as $\overset{\greenD1}{\blueD{6}} + \overset{\greenD2}{\blueD{6}} + \overset{\greenD3}{\blueD{6}} +\overset{\greenD4}{\blueD{6}} + \overset{\greenD5}{\blueD{6}} + \overset{\greenD6}{\blueD{6}} +\overset{\greenD7}{\blueD{6}}$.
We can also think of $\greenD7\blueD{\text{ sixes}} $ as $\greenD7$ groups of $\blueD6$.
We can write $\greenD7$ groups of $\blueD6$ with multiplication as $\greenD7\times\blueD6$.

The following expressions are equivalent to $\greenD{7}\blueD{\text{ sixes}}$:

- $\blueD6 + \blueD6 + \blueD6 + \blueD6 + \blueD6 + \blueD6 + \blueD6$
- $\greenD7\times\blueD6$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions are equal to $\greenD{2}\blueD{\text{ fours}}$?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints: $\greenD2\blueD{\text{ fours}} $ is the same as $\overset{\greenD1}{\blueD{4}} + \overset{\greenD2}{\blueD{4}}$.
We can also think of $\greenD2\blueD{\text{ fours}} $ as $\greenD2$ groups of $\blueD4$.
We can write $\greenD2$ groups of $\blueD4$ with multiplication as $\greenD2\times\blueD4$.

The following expressions are equivalent to $\greenD{2}\blueD{\text{ fours}}$:

- $\blueD4 + \blueD4$
- $\greenD2\times\blueD4$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions are equal to $8+8+8+8$ ?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints: $\overset{\greenD1}{\blueD{8}} + \overset{\greenD2}{\blueD{8}} + \overset{\greenD3}{\blueD{8}} + \overset{\greenD4}{\blueD{8}}$ is the same as $\green4\blueD{\text{ eights}}$.
We can also think of $\greenD4\blueD{\text{ eights}} $ as $\greenD4$ groups of $\blueD8$.
We can write $\greenD4$ groups of $\blueD8$ with multiplication as $\greenD4\times\blueD8$.

The following expressions are equivalent to $8+8+8+8$:

- $\greenD{4}\blueD{\text{ eights}}$
- $\greenD4\times\blueD8$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions are equal to $1+1+1+1+1+1$ ?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints: $\overset{\greenD1}{\blueD{1}} + \overset{\greenD2}{\blueD{1}} + \overset{\greenD3}{\blueD{1}} + \overset{\greenD4}{\blueD{1}} + \overset{\greenD5}{\blueD{1}} + \overset{\greenD6}{\blueD{1}}$ is the same as $\green6\blueD{\text{ ones}}$.
We can also think of $\greenD6\blueD{\text{ ones}} $ as $\greenD6$ groups of $\blueD1$.
We can write $\greenD6$ groups of $\blueD1$ with multiplication as $\greenD6\times\blueD1$.

The following expressions are equivalent to $1+1+1+1+1+1$:

- $\greenD{6}\blueD{\text{ ones}}$
- $\greenD6\times\blueD1$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions are equal to $10+10$ ?**

[[â˜ƒ radio 1]]
hints: $\overset{\greenD1}{\blueD{10}} + \overset{\greenD2}{\blueD{10}}$ is the same as $\green2\blueD{\text{ tens}}$.
We can also think of $\greenD2\blueD{\text{ tens}} $ as $\greenD2$ groups of $\blueD{10}$.
We can write $\greenD2$ groups of $\blueD{10}$ with multiplication as $\greenD2\times\blueD{10}$.

The following expressions are equivalent to $10+10$:

- $\greenD{2}\blueD{\text{ tens}}$
- $\greenD2\times\blueD{10}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the equivalent expressions.**

[[â˜ƒ matcher 2]]
hints: $\greenD3$ groups of $\blueD7$ is $\blueD7+\blueD7+\blueD7$.
$\goldD7+\goldD7+\goldD7+\goldD7$ is $\purpleC4\times\goldD7$.
$\maroonD3\times\tealD4$ is $\maroonD3\tealD{\text{ fours}}$.

The equivalent expressions are:


$$ | $$ | 
- | - | -
$3 \text{ groups of } 7$ | $7+7+7$ 
$7+7+7+7$ | $4\times7$ 
$3\times4$ | $3\text { fours}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the equivalent expressions.**

[[â˜ƒ matcher 2]]
hints: $\greenD2$ groups of $\blueD8$ is $\blueD8+\blueD8$.
$\goldD8+\goldD8+\goldD8+\goldD8+\goldD8$ is $\purpleC5\times\goldD8$.
$\maroonD2\times\tealD5$ is $\maroonD2\tealD{\text{ fives}}$.

The equivalent expressions are:


$$ | $$ | 
- | - | -
$2 \text{ groups of } 8$ | $8+8$ 
$8+8+8+8+8$ | $5\times8$ 
$2\times5$ | $2\text { fives}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the equivalent expressions.**

[[â˜ƒ matcher 2]]
hints: $\greenD2$ groups of $\blueD5$ is $\greenD2 \times\blueD5$.
$\goldD5+\goldD5+\goldD5$ is $\purpleC3\goldD{\text{ fives}}$.
$\tealD3$ groups of $\maroonD2$ is $\maroonD2+\maroonD2+\maroonD2$.

The equivalent expressions are:


$$ | $$ | 
- | - | -
$2 \text{ groups of } 5$ | $2\times 5$
$5+5+5$ | $3\text { fives}$ 
$3\text{ groups of } 2$ | $2+2+2$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the equivalent expressions.**

[[â˜ƒ matcher 2]]
hints: $\greenD6$ groups of $\blueD4$ is $\greenD6 \times\blueD4$.
$\goldD6+\goldD6+\goldD6 +\goldD6+\goldD6$ is $\purpleC5\goldD{\text{ sixes}}$.
$\tealD3$ groups of $\maroonD4$ is $\maroonD4+\maroonD4+\maroonD4$.

The equivalent expressions are:


$$ | $$ | 
- | - | -
$6 \text{ groups of } 4$ | $6\times4$
$6+6+6+6+6$ | $5\text { sixes}$ 
$3\text{ groups of } 4$ | $4+4+4$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the equivalent expressions.**

[[â˜ƒ matcher 2]]
hints: $\goldD9+\goldD9+\goldD9$ is $\purpleC3\goldD{\text{ nines}}$.
$\greenD9$ groups of $\blueD2$ is $\greenD9\times\blueD2$.
$\tealD2$ groups of $\maroonD3$ is $\maroonD3+\maroonD3$.

The equivalent expressions are:


$$ | $$ | 
- | - | -
$9+9+9$ | $3\text { nines}$
$9\text{ groups of } 2$ | $9\times2$ 
$2\text{ groups of } 3$ | $3+3$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the equivalent expressions.**

[[â˜ƒ matcher 2]]
hints: $\goldD8+\goldD8+\goldD8+\goldD8$ is $\purpleC4\goldD{\text{ eights}}$.
$\greenD8$ groups of $\blueD3$ is $\greenD8\times\blueD3$.
$\tealD4+\tealD4+\tealD4$ is $\maroonD3\times\tealD4$.

The equivalent expressions are:


$$ | $$ | 
- | - | -
$8+8+8+8$ | $4\text { eights}$
$8\text{ groups of } 3$ | $8\times3$ 
$4+4+4$ | $3\times4$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the equivalent expressions.**

[[â˜ƒ matcher 2]]
hints: $\goldD{10}+\goldD{10}+\goldD{10}+\goldD{10} +\goldD{10}$ is $\purpleC5\goldD{\text{ tens}}$.
$\greenD9 + \greenD9 + \greenD9 + \greenD9 + \greenD9$ is $\blueD{5}$ groups of $\greenD{9}$
$\tealD{10}$ groups of $\maroonD9$ is $\tealD{10}\times\maroonD9$.

The equivalent expressions are:

| | 
- | - | -
$10+10+10+10+10$ | $5\text { tens}$
$9+9+9+9+9$ | $5\text{ groups of } 9$
$10\text{ groups of } 9$ | $10\times9$
